formula: Subject + is/are + the + Adjective-est  |  Subject + is/are + the most + Adjective
formula_visual: [THING] + is + the + [ADJ]-est / the most + [ADJ] (in the group/world/family)
when_to_use: Use superlatives to compare THREE OR MORE things and identify the one at the extreme (highest or lowest) of a quality. Always use 'the' before a superlative. Short adjectives add -est; longer adjectives use 'the most'.
when_to_use_ar: استخدم صيغ التفضيل المطلق لمقارنة ثلاثة أشياء أو أكتر وتحديد اللي في القمة (الأعلى أو الأقل) في صفة معينة. استخدم the قبل صيغة التفضيل دايمًا.
why_arabic_speakers_struggle: Learners who just learned comparatives last week often try to reuse '-er + than' patterns for group comparisons ('the biggest than all'), or forget the required article 'the', which is grammatically mandatory before every English superlative and has no direct one-to-one equivalent trigger in Arabic superlative structures.

examples.en: This is the biggest city in the country.
She is the tallest person in her family.
This is the most expensive restaurant in town.
He is the most interesting person I know.
This was the worst day of my life.
examples.ar: ده كان أسوأ يوم في حياتي

common_errors.explanation: The article 'the' is mandatory before a superlative — it can never be dropped in English.

common_errors.wrong: This is the more expensive restaurant in town.
common_errors.correct: This is the most expensive restaurant in town.
common_errors.explanation: For 3+ things, long adjectives need 'the MOST', not 'the more' (which mixes comparative and superlative forms).

common_errors.wrong: She is the most tall person here.
common_errors.correct: She is the tallest person here.
common_errors.explanation: 'tall' is short (1 syllable) — it takes -est, not 'the most'.

practice_fill_blank.sentence: This is ___ (good) restaurant in the city.
He is ___ (funny) person in our group.
practice_fill_blank.hint: 2 syllables ending in y → change y to i, add -est.

practice_fill_blank.sentence: That was ___ (boring) movie I have ever seen.
This is ___ (small) phone in the store.
She is ___ (intelligent) student in the class.

quick_rule: ALWAYS use 'the' before a superlative — no exceptions. Short adjectives → the + adj-est. Long adjectives → the most + adj. Irregular: good→the best, bad→the worst, far→the farthest/furthest.
quick_rule_ar: استخدم the دايمًا قبل صيغة التفضيل — بدون استثناء. الصفات القصيرة → the + adj-est. الصفات الطويلة → the most + adj.
mnemonic: SUPERLATIVE = 'the SUPER one' — there's only ONE super winner, so it always needs 'THE' to point at that single one.
connect_to_speaking: Practice with your buddy: name the best restaurant, the worst movie, and the most interesting person you know, using full superlative sentences each time — never drop 'the'.
